title: Cambridge Challenge
seoDescription: Take a fast Cambridge-style reasoning quiz with staged logic, pattern, deduction, and word challenges.
pageTitle: Only 4% Can Pass This Cambridge Test
summary: A supervision-style reasoning challenge for sharp minds.

homepage.title: Can You Pass This Cambridge Entrance Test?
homepage.summary: A supervision-style reasoning challenge for sharp minds.

infoPanel.title: About This Cambridge Test
infoPanel.intro: This Cambridge-style quiz is a fast reasoning challenge inspired by the kind of clear thinking, pattern recognition, deduction, and careful reading often associated with college interviews and supervision-style discussion. It is not an official University of Cambridge exam, but it is designed to feel sharp, focused, and mentally demanding.

columns.title: How It Works
columns.body: The quiz begins with a short college warm-up, then moves through visual logic, attention, number patterns, word reasoning, deduction, lateral thinking, and final scholar questions. Each round is short, so the test stays quick while gradually becoming harder.

columns.title: About Cambridge Admissions
columns.body: Cambridge undergraduate admissions are competitive and course-specific. Depending on the subject, applicants may be assessed through academic achievement, written work, admissions tests, interviews, references, and personal statements. There is no single universal Cambridge entrance exam for every applicant.

infoPanel.footerBody: Every question in this quiz has one correct answer and an explanation. Your score is for entertainment and self-challenge only; it should not be treated as an official admissions result or academic assessment.

landing.challengeText: Most players never reach the final round.

profiles.title: You beat the test.
profiles.copy: Exceptional reasoning across the full challenge. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Elite reasoning.
profiles.copy: You stayed sharp through the harder rounds. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Sharp under pressure.
profiles.copy: Strong performance across the skill rounds, led by {stage}.

profiles.title: Above-average reasoning.
profiles.copy: You showed good instincts, especially in {stage}.

profiles.title: Solid score.
profiles.copy: You handled several rounds well. Your strongest area was {stage}.

profiles.title: Keep sharpening.
profiles.copy: The hardest rounds reward calm pattern memory. Your best area was {stage}.

stageGroups.title: College Warm-Up

questions.prompt: In the college list, which item is the crossing rather than water?
questions.explanation: River, canal, and stream are waterways. A bridge is the crossing over one.

questions.prompt: Complete the doubling sequence.
questions.explanation: Each number doubles: 2, 4, 8, 16, then 32.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">2   4   8   16   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Choose the only number divisible by 4.
questions.explanation: 36 is divisible by 4.

questions.prompt: If BOOK becomes CPPL, then PAGE becomes...
questions.explanation: Each letter moves forward by one.

questions.prompt: Which shape has fewer sides than a hexagon but more than a square?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has five sides, between a square’s four and a hexagon’s six.

questions.prompt: Pen is to writing as lens is to...
questions.explanation: A pen helps with writing; a lens helps with seeing.

questions.prompt: All fellows attend. Nora is a fellow. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If all fellows attend and Nora is a fellow, Nora attends.

questions.prompt: Which candidate word reads the same backwards?
questions.explanation: LEVEL reads the same from left to right and right to left.

stageGroups.encouragement: You cleared the college warm-up. Next comes supervision-style pattern speed.

stageGroups.title: Supervision Patterns

questions.prompt: Which number is smallest?
questions.explanation: 18 is the smallest number.

questions.prompt: Which comes next in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The seasons go spring, summer, autumn, then winter.
questions.visual: Spring→Summer→Autumn→?

questions.prompt: The sides increase by one each step. Which shape follows?
questions.explanation: The sequence moves from 3 sides to 4, then 5, then 6.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 96" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:96px"><polygon points="42,24 66,66 18,66" fill="#092f3a"/><rect x="92" y="27" width="42" height="42" rx="6" fill="#092f3a"/><polygon points="181,20 210,42 199,76 163,76 152,42" fill="#092f3a"/><text x="234" y="62" font-size="35" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which row breaks the rule?
questions.explanation: Row B contains a mixed symbol; the other rows repeat the same symbol.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 150" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:150px"><rect x="20" y="12" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f1f7f4"/><rect x="20" y="45" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f8fbfa"/><rect x="20" y="78" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f1f7f4"/><rect x="20" y="111" width="220" height="28" rx="10" fill="#f8fbfa"/><text x="130" y="34" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#092f3a">●  ●  ●</text><text x="130" y="67" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#092f3a">■  □  ■</text><text x="130" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#092f3a">▲  ▲  ▲</text><text x="130" y="133" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" fill="#092f3a">◆  ◆  ◆</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which symbol completes the college pattern row?
questions.explanation: The rows slide through a four-symbol cycle: star, circle, triangle, square. After triangle and square comes star.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 150" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:150px"><rect x="22" y="14" width="256" height="34" rx="10" fill="#f1f7f4"/><rect x="22" y="58" width="256" height="34" rx="10" fill="#f8fbfa"/><rect x="22" y="102" width="256" height="34" rx="10" fill="#f1f7f4"/><text x="150" y="39" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">★   ○   △</text><text x="150" y="83" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">○   △   □</text><text x="150" y="127" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">△   □   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: The arrow turns anticlockwise. Which direction follows?
questions.explanation: Moving anticlockwise from up gives left, then down, then right.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 88" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:88px"><text x="48" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="42" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">↑</text><text x="104" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="42" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">←</text><text x="160" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="42" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">↓</text><text x="216" y="55" text-anchor="middle" font-size="38" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which pair breaks the pattern?
questions.explanation: The first three pairs match. The final pair contains two different diamonds.

questions.prompt: Identify the next symbol in this repeating cycle.
questions.explanation: The cycle is square, circle, triangle, then repeats.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 280 92" style="width:280px;max-width:100%;height:92px"><rect x="20" y="30" width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#092f3a"/><circle cx="92" cy="46" r="16" fill="#092f3a"/><polygon points="151,25 171,64 131,64" fill="#092f3a"/><rect x="194" y="30" width="32" height="32" rx="6" fill="#092f3a"/><circle cx="260" cy="46" r="16" fill="#092f3a"/></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: Good pattern work. Next comes visual reasoning.

stageGroups.title: Visual Reasoning

questions.prompt: Which option finishes the size alternation?
questions.explanation: The squares alternate small and large, so the next one is large.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 260 92" style="width:260px;max-width:100%;height:92px"><rect x="28" y="37" width="18" height="18" rx="4" fill="#092f3a"/><rect x="73" y="25" width="42" height="42" rx="8" fill="#092f3a"/><rect x="142" y="37" width="18" height="18" rx="4" fill="#092f3a"/><text x="212" y="58" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which labelled image is the visual exception?
questions.explanation: D is a triangle, while the others are circles.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 86" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:86px"><text x="45" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">A</text><circle cx="45" cy="52" r="14" fill="#092f3a"/><text x="115" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">B</text><circle cx="115" cy="52" r="14" fill="#092f3a"/><text x="185" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">C</text><circle cx="185" cy="52" r="14" fill="#092f3a"/><text x="255" y="26" font-size="14" font-weight="900" fill="#667085">D</text><polygon points="255,34 272,66 238,66" fill="#092f3a"/></svg>

questions.prompt: Which missing shape preserves the pair rule?
questions.explanation: Each pair shows the same shape in black, then in white.

questions.prompt: Which shape comes after two circles?
questions.explanation: The pattern is triangle, circle, circle, square, then repeats.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 90" style="width:340px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="170" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="25" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">△   ○   ○   □   △   ○   ○   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which college word would be filed first alphabetically?
questions.explanation: Archive comes first alphabetically.

questions.prompt: How many letter E characters are in REFERENCE?
questions.explanation: REFERENCE contains four E characters.

questions.prompt: Choose the option with exactly six letters.
questions.explanation: Garden has exactly six letters.

questions.prompt: Which word is repeated in the notice?
questions.explanation: Green appears twice.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 95" style="width:330px;max-width:100%;height:95px"><text x="165" y="31" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">Red — Green — Blue — Amber</text><text x="165" y="63" text-anchor="middle" font-size="20" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">Black — Green</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: Nice focus. The next round checks close attention.

stageGroups.title: Detail Check

questions.prompt: If the instruction says choose option B, which option should you choose?
questions.explanation: The instruction explicitly says option B.

questions.prompt: Which number appears twice in the list?
questions.explanation: 628 appears twice.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 88" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:88px"><text x="160" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="23" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">314    628    752    628</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Ignore the smallest number. Which remaining number is largest?
questions.explanation: After ignoring 9, the largest remaining number is 71.

questions.prompt: Which pair is an exact match?
questions.explanation: RIVER / RIVER is the only identical pair.

questions.prompt: Which colour is written last?
questions.explanation: Red is the last of the three words shown.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 88" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:88px"><text x="160" y="44" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">Blue     Green     Red</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which answer has exactly two vowel letters?
questions.explanation: Stone contains two vowels: o and e.

questions.prompt: Add the steady interval to finish the sequence.
questions.explanation: The sequence adds 9 each time: 45, 54, 63, 72, 81.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">45   54   63   72   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Continue the growing-step sequence.
questions.explanation: The pattern adds 1, then 2, then 3, then 4, then 5.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">7   8   10   13   17   ?</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: Your score is building. Next comes number logic.

stageGroups.title: Number Logic

questions.prompt: Which square number completes the sequence?
questions.explanation: The numbers are square numbers: 4², 5², 6², then 7².
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">16   25   36   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Choose the next prime in the sequence.
questions.explanation: The sequence lists prime numbers, so after 13 comes 17.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">3   5   7   11   13   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which value replaces the missing term?
questions.explanation: The differences are +4, +6, +8, +10, then +12.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">7   11   17   25   35   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which number completes the doubling grid?
questions.explanation: Each row shows a number, double it, then quadruple it.

questions.prompt: If 4 books cost 28 units, what do 9 books cost?
questions.explanation: Each book costs 7 units, so 9 books cost 63 units.

questions.prompt: The average of 8, 12, and x is 15. What is x?
questions.explanation: The total must be 45. Since 8 + 12 = 20, x is 25.

questions.prompt: A value rises from 60 to 75. What is the percentage increase?
questions.explanation: The increase is 15 out of 60, which is 25%.

questions.prompt: Use the n by n plus two pattern to finish it.
questions.explanation: The terms are n × (n + 2): 1×3, 2×4, 3×5, and so on.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">3   8   15   24   35   ?</text></svg>

stageGroups.encouragement: Strong pace. The next round moves into word codes.

stageGroups.title: Word Codes

questions.prompt: If LATE becomes MBUF, then TIME becomes...
questions.explanation: Each letter shifts forward by one.

questions.prompt: Which letter follows the widening gaps?
questions.explanation: The gaps increase by 1: +1, +2, +3, +4, then +5.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">C   D   F   I   M   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which word is not part of the anagram set?
questions.explanation: Alert, Alter, and Later are anagrams. Stone is not.

questions.prompt: If LOGIC = 5 and COLLEGE = 7, then RIVER = ?
questions.explanation: The number equals the number of letters.

questions.prompt: If stressed becomes desserts, then reward becomes...
questions.explanation: The word is reversed.

questions.prompt: What letter is two letters before the letter after P?
questions.explanation: The letter after P is Q. Two letters before Q is O.

questions.prompt: Which word means the opposite of vague?
questions.explanation: Precise means clear and exact, the opposite of vague.

questions.prompt: Which phrase is most academically careful?
questions.explanation: The evidence suggests is cautious and precise.

stageGroups.encouragement: You're deep into the test now. Next comes deduction.

stageGroups.title: Deduction Round

questions.prompt: Hypothesis is to study as evidence is to...
questions.explanation: Evidence supports a conclusion.

questions.prompt: Which word is closest to “infer”?
questions.explanation: To infer is to conclude from evidence.

questions.prompt: All lums are naves. No naves are terns. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If every lum is a nave and no naves are terns, no lums can be terns.

questions.prompt: A is earlier than B. C is later than B. D is earlier than A. Who is earliest?
questions.explanation: D is earlier than A, and A is earlier than B and C.

questions.prompt: All essays are reviewed. All reviewed work receives notes. Therefore...
questions.explanation: If all essays are reviewed and all reviewed work receives notes, all essays receive notes.

questions.prompt: Some tutors are scientists. All scientists test claims. Therefore...
questions.explanation: The tutors who are scientists must test claims.

questions.prompt: Eli is before Priya. Priya is before Tomas. Who is last?
questions.explanation: The order is Eli, Priya, Tomas.

questions.prompt: I have pages but no paper, links but no chain, and searches but no detective. What am I?
questions.explanation: A website can have pages, links, and searches without physical paper or chains.

stageGroups.encouragement: Only focused players reach this point. Next comes lateral thinking.

stageGroups.title: Lateral Thinking

questions.prompt: The more you remove, the larger I become. What am I?
questions.explanation: Removing material makes a hole larger.

questions.prompt: What can travel around a room while staying in one corner?
questions.explanation: A stamp can travel on an envelope while staying in the corner of it.

questions.prompt: Every card in Box A is red. Some red cards are marked. Are all cards in Box A marked?
questions.explanation: Some red cards being marked does not prove all red cards are marked.

questions.prompt: Only registered guests may enter. Lena entered. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If only registered guests may enter and Lena entered, Lena must have been registered.

questions.prompt: A notebook and pen cost 1.10 units total. The notebook costs 1 unit more than the pen. What does the pen cost?
questions.explanation: If the pen costs 0.05, the notebook costs 1.05, totaling 1.10.

questions.prompt: You pass the person in third place. What place are you now in?
questions.explanation: Passing the person in third place puts you in third place.

questions.prompt: Three folders are labelled Drafts, Notes, Mixed. All labels are wrong. Which labelled folder should you open first?
questions.explanation: The Mixed label is wrong, so that folder contains only one type and reveals the others.

questions.prompt: Given: X equals Y. Y does not equal Z. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If X equals Y and Y differs from Z, then X differs from Z.

stageGroups.encouragement: Final stretch. Next comes the Cambridge interview round.

stageGroups.title: Cambridge Interview

questions.prompt: Final scholar pattern: which term follows?
questions.explanation: The differences are +4, +6, +8, +10, then +12, so the next term is 42.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 90" style="width:320px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="160" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">2   6   12   20   30   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: A clock gains 3 minutes every hour. If set correctly at noon, what time does it show after 5 real hours?
questions.explanation: It gains 15 minutes over 5 hours, so it shows 5:15.

questions.prompt: Complete the factorial sequence.
questions.explanation: This is the factorial sequence: 1!, 2!, 3!, 4!, 5!, then 6!.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">1   2   6   24   120   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Some A are B. All B are C. What must be true?
questions.explanation: The A that are B must also be C.

questions.prompt: If C = 3 and ACE = 9, then BED = ?
questions.explanation: Letters are converted to positions and added: B=2, E=5, D=4, total 11.

questions.prompt: What value completes the squared-sum pattern?
questions.explanation: Square both numbers and add them: 7² + 8² = 49 + 64 = 113.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 130" style="width:270px;max-width:100%;height:130px"><text x="135" y="26" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">4 + 5 = 41</text><text x="135" y="52" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">5 + 6 = 61</text><text x="135" y="78" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">6 + 7 = 85</text><text x="135" y="104" text-anchor="middle" font-size="21" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">7 + 8 = ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Continue the odd-increment number pattern.
questions.explanation: The pattern adds 5, then 7, then 9, then 11, then 13.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">33   38   45   54   65   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Which statement must be true? All prals are morks. No morks are sens.
questions.explanation: If every pral is a mork and no morks are sens, no prals can be sens.

stageGroups.encouragement: One last push. The final scholar round is ready.

questions.prompt: If CAMBRIDGE is coded as DBNCSJEHF, what happens to each letter?
questions.explanation: Each letter has moved forward by one.

questions.prompt: Which choice completes the alternating visual rule?
questions.explanation: The shape stays a circle while the fill alternates black, white, black, white.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 270 90" style="width:270px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><circle cx="42" cy="45" r="17" fill="#092f3a"/><circle cx="96" cy="45" r="17" fill="#eef3f8" stroke="#d7e0ea"/><circle cx="150" cy="45" r="17" fill="#092f3a"/><text x="214" y="56" font-size="34" font-weight="950" fill="#667085">?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Only answers with evidence are accepted. This answer was accepted. What must be true?
questions.explanation: If only answers with evidence are accepted, an accepted answer must have evidence.

questions.prompt: A student starts fourth and passes two people. What place are they in now?
questions.explanation: Passing two people from fourth moves the student to second place.

questions.prompt: Which number is not a cube?
questions.explanation: 64, 125, and 216 are cube numbers. 28 is not.

questions.prompt: What is the safest conclusion? Some readers are analysts. All analysts compare sources.
questions.explanation: The readers who are analysts must compare sources.

questions.prompt: Final letter pattern: which letter comes next?
questions.explanation: The gaps are +2, +3, +4, +5, then +6. After O comes U.
questions.visual: <svg viewBox="0 0 300 90" style="width:300px;max-width:100%;height:90px"><text x="150" y="45" text-anchor="middle" font-size="24" font-weight="950" fill="#092f3a">A   C   F   J   O   ?</text></svg>

questions.prompt: Final judgement: which conclusion stays within the evidence?
choices: The evidence makes the claim plausible
questions.explanation: A careful conclusion does not overstate the evidence. Plausible is the most cautious supported answer.
